title: Coverage and gate completeness scan
description: Find gaps where coverage or release gates claim comprehensive testing but omit packages, generated assets, hooks, scripts, thresholds, or required commands. Avoid duplicates of ISS-20260609-011 unless stronger/different evidence exists.
findings: Found 1 high-confidence gate completeness issue: rc6 gate can pass while omitting most CLI tests and documented CLI drift gates; appended one gate-completeness pattern to discoveries.ndjson.
issues_found: [{"title":"rc6 gate reports PASS while running only a CLI hook test subset","severity":"high","description":"The root rc6:gate script is the named release/completion gate, but scripts/rc6-coverage-gate.mjs intentionally targets only four CLI hook test files instead of the full @fenglimg/fabric-cli test suite, while still running server/shared tests and producing a PASS result. docs/TESTING.md lists the CLI surface snapshot as a drift gate, and the CLI test tree contains many non-hook suites, so CLI command/help/install/i18n drift can regress while rc6:gate remains green. This is stronger than the documented partial CLI surface limitation because the release gate omits that drift gate entirely.","location":"scripts/rc6-coverage-gate.mjs:496","fix_direction":"Make rc6:gate run the full @fenglimg/fabric-cli test suite or an explicit maintained CLI release-suite that includes cli-surface.test.ts and other public CLI drift/contract tests; if a subset is unavoidable, change the output/name to say it is a hook-only gate and add a separate required CLI gate in package.json/CI.","affected_components":["scripts/rc6-coverage-gate.mjs","package.json rc6:gate","packages/cli/__tests__","docs/TESTING.md drift gates"],"perspective":"coverage-and-gate-completeness"}]

title: Public workflow integration coverage scan
description: Find user-visible CLI/MCP/store/install/doctor/archive/review/sync flows that are only unit-tested or partially tested, missing full end-to-end assertions across package boundaries and real filesystem/config state.
findings: Found two non-duplicate public workflow integration coverage gaps: store switch-write misses the bindings-snapshot chain that hooks consume, and doctor mutation CLI tests mock the server instead of exercising real command+filesystem behavior.
issues_found: [{"title":"fabric store switch-write lacks end-to-end coverage for resolved-bindings snapshot refresh","severity":"high","description":"The public onboarding flow tells users to run `fabric store bind` and then `fabric store switch-write`, and hooks read the resolved-bindings snapshot for read/write labels. `bind` refreshes that snapshot, but the `switch-write` command only calls `storeSwitchWrite` and prints success; the helper test asserts only active/default write config fields. A regression can leave the snapshot write_target stale after changing the write store while tests pass.","location":"packages/cli/src/commands/store.ts:190","fix_direction":"Add a command-level integration test that seeds two mounted writable stores, runs the public `store switch-write` command, reads the resolved-bindings snapshot, and asserts `write_target.alias` changes. Update the command to call `regenerateBindingsSnapshot(projectRoot, ...)` after `storeSwitchWrite`; consider the same assertion for `route-write` when write routes affect snapshot resolution.","affected_components":["fabric store switch-write","resolved-bindings snapshot","knowledge hints/hooks","packages/cli/src/store/store-ops.ts","packages/shared/src/store/bindings.ts"],"perspective":"public-workflow-integration-coverage"},{"title":"doctor mutation commands lack real CLI-to-server filesystem integration coverage","severity":"medium","description":"The public `fabric doctor --fix` and `fabric doctor --fix-knowledge` branches call server mutation APIs, render summaries, set exit codes, and run extra CLI-side repairs, but CLI tests mock `@fenglimg/fabric-server` throughout while server tests call `runDoctorFix`/`runDoctorApplyLint` directly. This leaves no gate that runs the actual doctor command against a real temp project/store and verifies both filesystem mutation and CLI output/exit-code behavior across package boundaries.","location":"packages/cli/__tests__/doctor.test.ts:152","fix_direction":"Add one or two slower integration tests that import/run `doctorCommand` without mocking `@fenglimg/fabric-server`, seed a temp project/global store state with a deterministic fixable condition, invoke `--fix` or `--fix-knowledge --yes`, then assert on-disk changes, JSON/human output, store diagnostics, alias-link side effects, and `process.exitCode`.","affected_components":["fabric doctor --fix","fabric doctor --fix-knowledge","packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts","packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts","CLI/server package boundary"],"perspective":"public-workflow-integration-coverage"}]

title: Negative boundary and error matrix scan
description: Find untested or weakly tested invalid inputs, malformed JSON/JSONL, filesystem permission failures, stale lock/counter state, id collisions, concurrency, retry, and graceful degradation behavior. Avoid duplicates of ISS-20260609-007/008 unless stronger/different evidence exists.
findings: Found 1 new medium-severity testing gap: the events.jsonl G11 invariant test still preserves an rc.37 metric-event overlap in v2.2 instead of enforcing the documented zero-overlap post-cutover contract.
issues_found: [{"title":"Events JSONL G11 invariant test still asserts rc37 metric-counter overlap","severity":"medium","description":"events-jsonl-gates.ts documents G11 as the structural invariant that every METRIC_COUNTER_NAMES value must not appear in eventLedgerEventSchema, and the package is now version 2.2.0-rc.5. The sibling test still says the post-rc.38 cutover should flip to zero overlap but asserts expect(overlap.length).toBe(counterNames.size), so the suite stays green while the forbidden dual-write overlap remains and can mask regressions in metric/event separation.","location":"packages/server/src/services/events-jsonl-gates.test.ts:152","fix_direction":"Flip the G11 test to expect(overlap).toEqual([]) for the current v2.2 line, remove metric counter event types from the event ledger schema, and adjust the G8 leak test/implementation to detect raw metric-event rows without depending on those forbidden rows parsing as valid typed ledger events.","affected_components":["packages/server/src/services/events-jsonl-gates.test.ts","packages/server/src/services/events-jsonl-gates.ts","packages/shared/src/schemas/event-ledger.ts","packages/server/src/services/metrics.ts"],"perspective":"negative-boundary-and-error-matrix"}]

title: Fixture snapshot seed drift scan
description: Find stale fixtures, snapshots, docs/test-seed entries, golden outputs, and dogfood harnesses that preserve retired contracts or implementation noise instead of stable behavior. Avoid duplicates of ISS-20260609-005/009 and prior test-seed issues unless the finding is materially different.
findings: Found 2 fixture/snapshot drift issues: one medium stale dogfood fixture that still treats project-local .fabric/knowledge as production-shaped test data, and one low orphan snapshot preserving retired plan_context archive-copy text.
issues_found: [{"title":"Werewolf snapshot fixture still pins project-local .fabric knowledge as production-shaped test data","severity":"medium","description":"The werewolf fixture test asserts that a tarred project-local .fabric tree still contains agents.meta.json and .fabric/knowledge, then runs doctor against that layout; the policy file calls the same tarball a realistic production-shaped knowledge base, and the CLI cross-client parity smoke test reuses it. In the store-only era this golden fixture lets doctor/parity tests stay green without exercising mounted store config, store.json, or cross-store read/write resolution, so fixture updates can preserve the retired co-location contract instead of the current user workflow.","location":"packages/server/__tests__/werewolf-fixture.test.ts:49","fix_direction":"Regenerate the dogfood fixture as a mounted-store project: create a store with store.json under an isolated Fabric home, bind it via .fabric/fabric-config.json, place knowledge entries in the store, and assert doctor/parity behavior through the store resolver. If the old tarball is retained, mark it legacy-only and stop using it as the production-shaped baseline.","affected_components":["packages/server/__tests__/werewolf-fixture.test.ts","packages/server/__tests__/__fixtures__/werewolf-snapshot.tar.gz","packages/cli/__tests__/cross-client-parity.test.ts","doctor fixture coverage","mounted store workflow"],"perspective":"fixture-snapshot-seed-drift"},{"title":"Orphan archive-hint snapshot preserves retired plan_context copy with no test owner","severity":"low","description":"packages/cli/__tests__/integration/__snapshots__/archive-hint-copy.test.ts.snap still contains archive reminder goldens phrased around accumulated plan_context calls, but rg --files finds no matching archive-hint-copy.test.ts/js owner. Vitest will ignore the orphan snapshot, leaving stale retired-contract output in the repo with no active gate to force either deletion or an update to the current edit-count/fab_recall-era wording.","location":"packages/cli/__tests__/integration/__snapshots__/archive-hint-copy.test.ts.snap:6","fix_direction":"Delete the orphan snapshot, or restore a real semantic test that asserts the current archive signal wording and source, then add a small orphan-snapshot check so .snap files cannot outlive their owning tests.","affected_components":["packages/cli/__tests__/integration/__snapshots__/archive-hint-copy.test.ts.snap","archive hint copy snapshots","snapshot hygiene"],"perspective":"fixture-snapshot-seed-drift"}]

title: Flaky isolation and test infrastructure scan
description: Find test infrastructure weaknesses that can make tests flaky or environment-dependent: real home/global state leaks, untracked worktree scanning, clock/timezone/process/env coupling, shared temp dirs, network/process assumptions, and platform-specific skips without alternate coverage.
findings: Found three non-duplicate flaky/isolation issues: one real p95 timing gate, one global FABRIC_PROJECT_ROOT leak from HTTP app construction, and one worktree-local temp-dir pattern in config-loader tests.
issues_found: [{"title":"Shared recall perf test gates on real p95 timing","severity":"medium","description":"The shared store recall perf test measures 30 real iterations with performance.now() and fails if read-set p95 exceeds full-scan p95 * 1.2. The structural read-set behavior is already asserted separately, but this timing assertion can fail from CPU, cache, GC, or filesystem jitter and make the package test gate environment-dependent.","location":"packages/shared/test/store/recall-perf.test.ts:83","fix_direction":"Keep the structural assertions that prove only the read-set is scanned, and move timing to a non-blocking benchmark/report script or use injected counters/instrumentation instead of wall-clock p95 in Vitest.","affected_components":["packages/shared/test/store/recall-perf.test.ts","@fenglimg/fabric-shared test gate"],"perspective":"flaky-isolation-and-test-infra"},{"title":"HTTP app tests inherit a global FABRIC_PROJECT_ROOT mutation","severity":"medium","description":"createFabricHttpApp writes process.env.FABRIC_PROJECT_ROOT during app construction, while its dispose path only clears timers/watchers and the integration test afterEach only calls dispose and removes the temp dir. Later tests in the same worker can inherit a deleted temp project root, making behavior order-dependent when code falls back to process.env.FABRIC_PROJECT_ROOT.","location":"packages/server-http-experimental/src/http.ts:247","fix_direction":"Avoid mutating process.env in createFabricHttpApp; pass projectRoot explicitly to session/server creation. If a temporary env bridge is still required, save the prior value and restore it in dispose, and add an integration test that asserts FABRIC_PROJECT_ROOT is restored after dispose.","affected_components":["packages/server-http-experimental/src/http.ts","packages/server-http-experimental/__tests__/integration/http-endpoints.test.ts","MCP HTTP test harness"],"perspective":"flaky-isolation-and-test-infra"},{"title":"config-loader tests create temp fixtures inside the real worktree","severity":"low","description":"config-loader.test.ts creates per-test directories under process.cwd() such as .tmp-config-loader-tests using Date.now()/Math.random(), then removes only the current tempDir in afterEach. An interrupted test run leaves untracked fixture directories in the repository, which can pollute later git/worktree scans and local developer state.","location":"packages/server/src/config-loader.test.ts:19","fix_direction":"Use mkdtemp under os.tmpdir() for these fixtures, or create one tracked suite temp root outside the repository and remove it in afterAll; avoid writing throwaway test data under process.cwd().","affected_components":["packages/server/src/config-loader.test.ts","@fenglimg/fabric-server test fixtures","developer worktree hygiene"],"perspective":"flaky-isolation-and-test-infra"}]

title: Dedup + Issue Creation
description: Aggregate all wave-1 findings. Deduplicate against existing issues and within this run by file path + description similarity or same file:line, keep higher severity, assign collision-safe ISS-YYYYMMDD-NNN records, append source=discover issues to .workflow/issues/issues.jsonl and this session discovery-issues.jsonl.
findings: pre-dedup 9 -> post-dedup created 9. Appended ISS-20260609-012..020 to master and session registries; no existing same title/file:line/root-cause duplicates found. Distribution high=2, medium=5, low=2.
